# Task 2 — AI-driven dev setup

## Goal
Set the project up so AI assistants (Claude Code et al.) have authoritative, up-to-date guidance, and so static analysis catches drift before it lands. The project leans heavily on AI for implementation — this task is the "rails" that keep it on track.

## Prerequisites
- Task 1 (deps installed)

## Plan

### Agent guidance — single CLAUDE.md, framework rules via skills
**Decision**: only one project-level guidance file (`CLAUDE.md`). Framework usage rules live exclusively in Claude Code skills generated by `mix usage_rules.sync`. No `AGENTS.md` in this repo.

- [x] **`CLAUDE.md`** — slim, project-specific only (~100 lines). Contains:
  - General guidance (Ash First, Code Generation, Logs & Tests)
  - Tidewave MCP tools usage
  - AshAI tools usage
  - Project orientation (module names, scope-of-truth pointer)
  - Project conventions (multitenancy via `Exhs.Scope`, code-interface-first, HTTP via Req, mix conventions, migrations)
  - "Do not" list (raw Ecto, `String.to_atom/1`, DaisyUI, `require_atomic? false`, inline scripts, `@apply`, etc.)
  - Phoenix 1.8 specifics for this codebase
  - Tailwind v4 syntax for this codebase
  - List of useful skills and mix tasks
- [x] **`AGENTS.md` deleted** — its hand-written project bits were merged into CLAUDE.md; its synced phoenix usage rules now live solely in the `phoenix-framework` skill
- [x] **`mix.exs` `usage_rules` config cleaned** — only the `skills:` block remains; no file inlining
- [x] **Skills regenerated** via `mix usage_rules.sync --yes`:
  - `.claude/skills/ash-framework/` — Ash + ash_* usage rules
  - `.claude/skills/phoenix-framework/` — Phoenix + phoenix_* usage rules
- [x] CI check: `mix usage_rules.sync --yes && git diff --exit-code .claude/skills/` (no `--check` flag; sync+diff is the approach)
- [x] When new ash_*/phoenix_* deps are added, agents must re-run `mix usage_rules.sync --yes` (added to CLAUDE.md + CI catches drift)

### Credo
- [x] Generate baseline `.credo.exs` via `mix credo.gen.config`
- [x] Tighten ruleset: enabled `StrictModuleLayout`, `UtcNowTruncate`, `FilterReject`, `MapMap`, `RejectFilter`, `PassAsyncInTestCases`, `IoPuts`, `NegatedIsNil`, `LeakyEnvironment`, `MapGetUnsafePass`, `MixEnv`, `UnsafeToAtom`
- [x] `TagTODO` exit_status 0 (TODOs surfaced but don't fail CI; real work tracked in `tasks/`)
- [x] `LazyLogging` disabled (false positive on Elixir 1.7+)
- [x] Addressed 11/16 warnings in skeleton:
  - `@moduledoc false` added to Ash domain/resources and other generated modules
  - alias order in `exhs_web.ex`
  - `use`-before-`import` in `live_user_auth.ex`
  - `async: true` on `page_controller_test.exs`
  - aliased nested modules in `live_user_auth.ex`
- [x] Cleared remaining nested-module-alias warnings: aliased `Phoenix.HTML.Form` in `core_components.ex` and `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox` in `test/support/data_case.ex`
- [x] CI credo step is now required (removed `continue-on-error`); only intentional TODO tags remain (`exit_status: 0`)

### Dialyzer (deferred)
Not setting up yet — revisit later. `dialyxir` dep stays in `mix.exs` for when we're ready.

### Formatter & linting
- [x] `.formatter.exs` already imports Ash, Phoenix, AshPhoenix, etc. (Task 1)
- [x] `mix format --check-formatted` in CI (Task 1)

### ex_slop
- [x] Configure `ex_slop` per its README: plugin registered, 30 checks enabled automatically, plus recommended Credo built-ins (`AppendSingleItem`, `DoubleBooleanNegation`) added to enabled list
- [x] Runs via `mix credo --strict` — same as the existing Credo step (CI + `mix precommit`)
- [x] Added "Code quality" section to CLAUDE.md summarizing what ExSlop enforces

### Pre-commit (optional)
- [x] **Decision: CI-only** — no local pre-commit hooks. CI catches everything; CLAUDE.md instructs agents to run format + credo before finishing.

### Claude Code skills / config
- [x] Skills audit: `ash-framework`, `phoenix-framework` are the only project-local skills, both auto-managed
- [x] Expand `.claude/settings.local.json` allow-list with common safe commands (`mix help *`, `mix compile *`, `mix format *`, `mix test *`, `mix ash.*`, `mix phx.gen.*`, `mix usage_rules.*`, etc.)
- [x] CLAUDE.md already lists useful skills agents should reach for

## Open decisions
- [x] **Pre-commit vs CI-only** — decided CI-only, no local hooks
- [x] **Credo strictness level** — strict from day 1 (already running `--strict` in CI)
- [x] **Dialyzer in CI** — deferred; not in scope for this task. Revisit later.
- [x] **Sync drift detection** — `mix usage_rules.sync --yes && git diff --exit-code .claude/skills/` in CI

## Decided
- **Single agent file** — `CLAUDE.md` (project-specific). No `AGENTS.md`.
- **Framework rules channel** — Claude Code skills generated by `mix usage_rules.sync`. No inline framework rules in `CLAUDE.md`.
- **CI-only checks** — no local pre-commit hooks. Agents run `mix format` + `mix credo --strict` before finishing.
- **Credo strict from day 1** — `--strict` in CI and `mix precommit`.
- **Dialyzer deferred** — not in this task's scope.
- **Skills drift** — CI runs `mix usage_rules.sync --yes && git diff --exit-code .claude/skills/`.

## Done when
- `mix credo --strict`, `mix format --check-formatted`, and a skill-drift check all pass in CI
- `CLAUDE.md` stays small and project-specific
- Skills are auto-regenerable; CI catches when they're stale
- A new contributor (human or AI) can read `CLAUDE.md` + `tasks/00-plan.md` and orient in <10 minutes
